title: Paranomus dregei
intro: Paranomus dregei, also known as the Dune Clanemont or Clanemont, is a flowering plant species within the genus Paranomus, belonging to the family Proteaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paranomus dregei include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea infusions and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Paranomus dregei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. This family consists of approximately 1,674 species of plants, most of which are native to the southern hemisphere. The plant is further categorized into the subfamily Proteoideae, which comprises of about 800 species. The genus Paranomus is a part of this subfamily, comprising several species of plants. Paranomus dregei is an endemic plant found in the Western Cape province of South Africa.
